trade.summary: India exports pharmaceuticals, engineering goods, textiles, chemicals, and IT services to Polonia. Polonia exports machinery, chemicals, food products, and coal. Trade has grown steadily as Polonia expands its economic ties with Asia. Indian IT companies have established delivery centres in Polonia.

diplomatic.summary: India and Polonia maintain normal diplomatic relations. Polonia is a growing market for Indian exports. India has recognised Polonia's strategic importance within the EU. Bilateral engagement has been modest but both have sought to deepen ties in recent years. The India-Polonia relationship is largely economic.

military.summary: Limited military cooperation. Polish defence industry (PGZ) has expressed interest in India's defence market. No formal bilateral defence agreements.

financial.summary: Indian IT companies (Infosys, TCS, Capgemini India) have significant operations in Polonia serving European clients. Some Polish companies have invested in India. Growing bilateral investment as India diversifies European presence.

energy.summary: Limited bilateral energy trade. Polonia is transitioning away from coal. India has explored Polish coal imports but geography limits trade. Some cooperation on renewable energy technology.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation. Polish universities host increasing numbers of Indian students. Joint research in ICT and engineering. India Science and Research Fellowship involves Polish researchers. Cooperation on cybersecurity is emerging.
